```py back/egreen/lib/device/sensor/management/commands/compute_first_data_date.py
import logging
import arrow
from datetime import datetime
from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand

from egreen.lib.device.sensor.models import Sensor, Data

# Get an instance of a logger
logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


class Command(BaseCommand):
    """Retreive data from current cost via xively."""

    def handle(self, **options):
        """Method launch when command is launch."""
        sensors = Sensor.objects.filter(
            is_virtual=False,
            first_data_date__isnull=True,
        )
        

        for sensor in sensors:
            res = Data.get_first_and_last_data_date([sensor])

            first_date = None
            if res["first"] is not None:
                first_date = arrow.get(res["first"], "YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss").date()
            # first_data = Data.objects.filter(sensor=sensor)\
            #                          .order_by('rec_date', 'rec_time')\
            #                          .first()
            if first_date:
                Sensor.objects.filter(id=sensor.id).update(first_data_date=first_date)
            # if first_data:
            #     first_data_date = arrow.get(
            #         datetime.combine(first_data.rec_date, first_data.rec_time),
            #         'Europe/Paris'
            #     ).to('UTC')
            #     first_data_date = first_data_date.isoformat()
            #     Sensor.objects.filter(id=sensor.id)\
            #                   .update(first_data_date=first_data_date)
```

Ajouter des options en cli pour prendre le model de capteurs en arguments et un bàtiment parent. Ces arguments sont optionnels
Je vais vous aider à ajouter des options CLI optionnelles pour filtrer par modèle de capteur et bâtiment parent.

<patch>
<title>Add optional CLI args for sensor model and building</title>

<edit>
<path>back/egreen/lib/device/sensor/management/commands/compute_first_data_date.py</path>
<description>Add add_arguments method to define new CLI options</description>
<operation>insert_after</operation>
<old_text>class Command(BaseCommand):
    """Retreive data from current cost via xively."""</old_text>
<new_text>
    
    def add_arguments(self, parser):
        """Add command arguments."""
        parser.add_argument(
            '--model',
            dest='model',
            help='Filter sensors by model name',
        )
        parser.add_argument(
            '--building',
            dest='building',
            help='Filter sensors by parent building ID',
        )</new_text>
</edit>

<edit>
<path>back/egreen/lib/device/sensor/management/commands/compute_first_data_date.py</path>
<description>Update the handle method to use the new filters</description>
<operation>update</operation>
<old_text>        sensors = Sensor.objects.filter(
            is_virtual=False,
            first_data_date__isnull=True,
        )</old_text>
<new_text>        # Build base queryset
        sensors = Sensor.objects.filter(
            is_virtual=False,
            first_data_date__isnull=True,
        )

        # Apply filters if provided
        if options.get('model'):
            sensors = sensors.filter(model__name=options['model'])
            
        if options.get('building'):
            sensors = sensors.filter(device__building__id=options['building'])</new_text>
</edit>

<edit>
<path>back/egreen/lib/device/sensor/management/commands/compute_first_data_date.py</path>
<description>Update docstring to document new options</description>
<operation>update</operation>
<old_text>    """Retreive data from current cost via xively."""</old_text>
<new_text>    """
    Compute first data date for sensors.
    
    Optional arguments:
        --model: Filter sensors by model name
        --building: Filter sensors by parent building ID
    """</new_text>
</edit>

</patch>

Ces modifications permettent :

1. L'ajout de deux options CLI optionnelles :
   - `--model` pour filtrer par nom de modèle de capteur
   - `--building` pour filtrer par ID du bâtiment parent

2. La mise à jour de la méthode handle pour appliquer ces filtres si ils sont fournis

3. La mise à jour de la documentation pour inclure les nouvelles options

Vous pouvez maintenant utiliser la commande comme ceci :
```bash
# Tous les capteurs
python manage.py compute_first_data_date

# Filtrer par modèle
python manage.py compute_first_data_date --model="ModelName"

# Filtrer par bâtiment
python manage.py compute_first_data_date --building="123"

# Les deux filtres
python manage.py compute_first_data_date --model="ModelName" --building="123"
```
